Container.Name: boltarr
Container.Overview: Boltarr is a self-hosted network dashboard for mapping, monitoring, and analyzing your local network. Scan subnets with nmap, visualize your topology as an interactive graph, track devices, manage VLANs, and chat with an optional AI assistant that has full context of your network. AI is optional — works without it. Supports Ollama, OpenAI-compatible APIs, and Anthropic.
Container.Description: Boltarr is a self-hosted network dashboard for mapping, monitoring, and analyzing your local network.[br][br] [b]Features:[/b][br] - Interactive topology graph with drag-and-drop layout and VLAN coloring[br] - nmap-powered subnet scanning and host discovery[br] - Device management — add, edit, merge hosts, annotate with notes[br] - Services registry — track running services per host[br] - VLAN management — define VLANs and visualize them on the topology[br] - AI assistant — per-host and network-wide analysis (optional)[br][br] [b]AI providers supported:[/b] Ollama (local), OpenAI-compatible APIs, Anthropic Claude.[br] AI is fully optional — the app works without it.[br][br] [b]Data[/b] is stored in the mapped /app/data directory (SQLite + config.yaml).

Config.Name: Web UI Port
Config.Description: Port Boltarr listens on. Access the UI at http://[server-ip]:[port]/

Config.Name: Data
Config.Description: Persistent storage for the database (boltarr.db) and AI config (config.yaml).

Config.Name: AI Provider
Config.Description: AI provider to use. Leave blank to configure via the UI instead. Options: none | ollama | openai | anthropic

Config.Name: AI Base URL
Config.Description: Base URL for the AI API. Required for Ollama and OpenAI-compatible providers. Example: http://192.168.1.10:11434

Config.Name: AI Model
Config.Description: Default model to use. Example: llama3.2, gpt-4o, claude-haiku-4-5-20251001

Config.Name: AI API Key
Config.Description: API key for OpenAI-compatible or Anthropic providers. Not needed for Ollama.

Config.Name: AI Timeout (seconds)
Config.Description: Timeout in seconds for standard AI requests.

Config.Name: AI Long Timeout (seconds)
Config.Description: Timeout in seconds for long-running AI requests (e.g. full network analysis).
